project.name: LLM Gateway
project.description: LLM API security proxy gateway — intercepts, scans, and audits all OpenAI-compatible API requests

sprints.name: Phase 1: Core Proxy MVP
sprints.goal: Build a working OpenAI-compatible proxy that forwards chat completions (non-streaming + streaming SSE), embeddings, and models requests to upstream providers, driven by config-based routing

features.title: Project scaffold & Fastify entrypoint
features.description: Initialize TypeScript project with Fastify 5, configure tsconfig (strict mode), vitest, pnpm, and create the entrypoint src/index.ts with a health-check route. Set up project directory structure per DESIGN.md.
acceptance_criteria: Given the project is cloned, when `pnpm install && pnpm dev` is run, then Fastify starts and responds to GET /health with 200
TypeScript strict mode compiles without errors
Vitest test runner executes (even with 0 tests)
Directory structure matches DESIGN.md layout
features.technical_notes: Use pnpm 10, tsx for dev runtime, tsc for build. Fastify register pattern for route organization. Add .env.example with placeholder vars.

features.title: Configuration system (zod + .env)
features.description: Implement config parsing with zod: read .env and optional config file, validate provider definitions (name, baseUrl, apiKey, model aliases). Export typed config object for use across the app.
acceptance_criteria: Given a valid .env with PROVIDERS config, when the app starts, then config is parsed into a typed object
Given an invalid config (missing required field), when the app starts, then it exits with a clear zod error message
Provider entries contain: name, baseUrl, apiKey, modelMappings (alias -> real model name)
.env.example documents all config variables
features.technical_notes: Use dotenv for .env loading. Define zod schema for PORT, LOG_LEVEL, PROVIDERS (JSON string or separate vars). Provider config is the foundation for routing.

features.title: Model-to-provider router
features.description: Implement the router that resolves a requested model name to an upstream provider (baseUrl + apiKey). Support model alias mapping from config and pass-through for unknown models to a default provider.
acceptance_criteria: Given a request for model 'gpt-4o' which maps to provider 'openai', when routing, then the correct baseUrl and apiKey are returned
Given a request for an aliased model 'fast-chat' mapped to 'claude-3-haiku', when routing, then the resolved model name is 'claude-3-haiku' on the mapped provider
Given a model not in any mapping, when a default provider is configured, then it routes to the default provider with the original model name
Given no matching provider and no default, when routing, then return a 404-style error
features.technical_notes: Pure function, no I/O. Config-driven: router reads from the parsed provider config. This is the backbone for all proxy routes.

features.title: Non-streaming chat completions proxy
features.description: Implement POST /v1/chat/completions that forwards the request body to the upstream provider determined by the router. Handle request/response transformation, error forwarding, and correct OpenAI-compatible response headers.
acceptance_criteria: Given a valid chat completions request with stream:false, when proxied, then the upstream response is returned to the client with identical JSON structure
Given the upstream returns an error (4xx/5xx), when proxied, then the error is forwarded with correct status code and OpenAI error format
Given a request with a model alias, when proxied, then the model name is resolved before forwarding
Response includes correct Content-Type and timing headers
features.technical_notes: Use native fetch for upstream calls. This is the baseline proxy before adding streaming complexity. Keep forwarder generic — it should work for any OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

features.title: Streaming SSE chat completions proxy
features.description: Extend the chat completions proxy to support stream:true. Implement SSE parsing with eventsource-parser via Web Streams TransformStream, handling partial line buffering across TCP packets and the data: [DONE] special case. Relay SSE chunks to the client in real-time.
acceptance_criteria: Given a chat completions request with stream:true, when proxied, then SSE chunks are relayed to the client in real-time with correct content-type: text/event-stream
Given partial TCP packets, when the SSE parser buffers, then lines are correctly reassembled before processing
Given 'data: [DONE]' from upstream, when parsed, then it is forwarded as-is and the stream closes cleanly
Given an upstream error mid-stream, when it occurs, then an SSE error event is sent to the client before closing
Streaming latency overhead is < 5ms compared to direct upstream connection
features.technical_notes: This is the core technical challenge per DESIGN.md. Use TransformStream with a line buffer. Parse with eventsource-parser. The transform hook point is where PII scanning will plug in later (Phase 3).

features.title: /v1/models passthrough
features.description: Implement GET /v1/models that forwards to the upstream provider. Aggregate models from all configured providers, prefixing with provider name or returning a unified list.
acceptance_criteria: Given a GET /v1/models request, when proxied, then a list of available models is returned in OpenAI format
Given multiple providers configured, when models are listed, then models from all providers are included
Response matches OpenAI /v1/models response schema (object with 'data' array of model objects)
features.technical_notes: Can aggregate from multiple providers or just proxy to a single default. Keep it simple for MVP — aggregate is preferred but single-provider passthrough is acceptable.

features.title: /v1/embeddings passthrough
features.description: Implement POST /v1/embeddings that forwards embedding requests to the upstream provider. Similar pattern to non-streaming chat completions but for the embeddings endpoint.
acceptance_criteria: Given a valid embeddings request, when proxied, then the upstream response is returned with correct embedding vectors
Given an upstream error, when proxied, then the error is forwarded with correct status
Given a model alias for embeddings, when proxied, then the model name is resolved before forwarding
features.technical_notes: Re-use the forwarder from s1-feat-004. Embeddings is a simpler endpoint (no streaming variant), good place to validate the generic forwarder works for multiple endpoints.

features.title: End-to-end integration test
features.description: Write vitest integration tests that spin up the gateway and a mock upstream server. Verify the full request lifecycle: non-streaming chat, streaming SSE chat, models listing, and embeddings. Include error scenarios.
acceptance_criteria: Integration test suite starts a real Fastify instance and a mock upstream
Non-streaming chat completions are proxied correctly (request body forwarded, response returned)
Streaming SSE chat completions relay chunks in order with [DONE] termination
Error responses from upstream are forwarded with correct status codes
All tests pass with `pnpm test`
features.technical_notes: Use vitest with a mock HTTP server (e.g., Fastify instance as mock upstream). Test the full Fastify lifecycle, not individual functions in isolation. This validates the entire Phase 1 pipeline.
